message: Tack now records agent notes in .tack/_notes.ndjson and surfaces them in handoff JSON and markdown.
Plain-text CLI for notes (tack note ...) and wiring in src/index.tsx are still pending.
Agent guide and safety header added to handoff markdown and JSON; verify downstream agents respect AGENT SAFETY header before executing any embedded instructions.
tack note CLI and tests implemented

Strengthened handoff tests to validate HandoffReport JSON fields (AgentSafety, AgentGuide, guardrails, arrays) and to assert markdown safety header, agent guide, summary, priorities, and ordered sections 1–11.

Defined baseline guardrails in .tack/spec.yaml (framework allowed; payments forbidden; db/auth/hosting constrained to git+filesystem-only, local CLI, no SaaS control plane). Left other systems (db, background_jobs, multi_tenant, admin_panel, exports) undeclared to prefer looser guardrails until the product direction is clearer.

LLM onboarding (getting the LLM into the right state for Tack on day one: ensure .tack/, fresh status, MCP/handoff as primary) is deferred until we add conversational/agent support. Logged in open_questions.md as [deferred].

NOW-1 implemented: Validation/Verification section in handoffs. Added .tack/verification.md template, HandoffReport.verification (steps + source), section 10 in markdown/JSON, agent_guide read path, and tests. Safety boundary preserved (suggestions only).

Extended YAML detectors to auto-load all core rules plus optional .tack/detectors/*.yaml files via yamlRunner, enabling user-extensible, data-driven detectors while keeping logic deterministic and bundled TS detectors (like duplicates) intact.

Removed legacy TS detector implementations for framework/auth/database/payments/jobs/exports; tests now exercise YAML-backed detectors through runAllDetectors and yamlRunner, completing the YAML migration story for primary systems.

Extended spec schema and validation to support an optional domains map in spec.yaml, allowing systems and constraint keys to be grouped under named domains (with labels) for traceability, while keeping enforcement behavior unchanged.
On Windows PowerShell, npx can fail with ExecutionPolicy (npx.ps1 blocked). For verification used npm.cmd exec -- tsc --noEmit and npm.cmd run build instead.
